agilitycompetition

Class Dog

  • java.lang.Object
    • agilitycompetition.Dog
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.lang.Comparable<Dog>
    Direct Known Subclasses:
    Pet

    public class Dog
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.lang.Comparable<Dog>
    Course: CSCI 160
    Project: AgilityCompetition (Version 2 Solution)
    File: Dog.java
    Uses: Date class
    Extends: nothing
    Implements: Comparable
    Presumption: All dogs have these attributes
    A class to represent a Dog
    Author:
    aapplin

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        toString allows an object to be directly printed by returning a String that can be printed to the console or to a file.
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        a formatted string representing the values of the attributes for a dog object.

      • compareTo

        public int compareTo(Dog that)
        compareTo (abstract method of the Comparable Interface) is implemented to impose a natural ordering on a group of objects. compareTo is used by the Collections.sort routine to allow us to sort the competitors belonging to some Java collection. Normally, a method would only have a single return statement. This method cannot be rewritten that way. Conditions are check in decreasing importance and as soon as a criteria fits we are finished looking at the comparison.
        Specified by:
        compareTo in interface java.lang.Comparable<Dog>
        Parameters:
        that - is the dog object we are comparing this one to
        Returns:
        a negative integer, zero, or a positive integer if this object comes before, is equal to, or comes after the specified object.

      • main

        public static void main(java.lang.String[] args)
        Unit Test for the dog class.
        Parameters:
        args - the command line arguments
